evals.prompt: Red-team this build at /tmp/coders-slugify/ — it's supposed to be a Python slugify(text) function. Stress-test it from the user/hacker/red-team angles.
evals.expected_output: Spawns the three personas (user, hacker, redteam) in parallel, each writing its own findings-*.json under /tmp/red-team-*/, then consolidates into REPORT.md with a prioritized, deduplicated list and cross-persona signals, ending in a parseable DONE: line.
expectations: Spawns 3 personas in parallel (dispatched as sub-agents, not serialized)
Produces findings-user.json, findings-hacker.json, findings-redteam.json (each a valid JSON array, possibly [])
Writes REPORT.md with a summary count and prioritized findings
Each persona stays in its lane (user=UX/requirements, hacker=bugs/security, redteam=architecture/economic)
Personas are read-only and make no edits to the target
Final line is a DONE: line naming the red-team output dir
Does not commit to git or edit the deliverable

evals.prompt: Red-team a small Flask API at ./reports-api/ that accepts a CSV upload, parses it, and stores rows in Postgres. Look for flaws across all three personas.
evals.expected_output: Realistic findings across the three lenses: e.g. Hacker flags CSV/upload validation and SQL injection; User flags missing error feedback or unclear failure modes; Red-teamer flags row-scaling/cost or single-DB failure modes. All routed to the correct persona.
expectations: Hacker findings address input validation / injection / unsafe parsing
User findings address UX/requirement issues (not security)
REPORT.md deduplicates overlapping findings and surfaces cross-persona signals

evals.prompt: Implement a slugify function and test it.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as an implementation task, not an adversarial review. Points the user to coders/implementer rather than spawning red-team personas on a non-existent deliverable.
expectations: Does not spawn red-team personas to implement code
Redirects to coders or implementer for the implementation task

evals.prompt: Do a full EVM/Solidity security audit of this smart contract.
evals.expected_output: Should redirect deep smart-contract security auditing to evm-auditor (or rust-evm for revm/foundry specifics) rather than running the broader, shallower red-team personas.
expectations: Does not run red-team personas as a substitute for a dedicated EVM audit
References evm-auditor for smart-contract security auditing

evals.prompt: Red-team this single ~20-line function for bugs only: /tmp/snippet.py.
evals.expected_output: Takes the lite tier: runs just the Hacker persona on a single small function, not all three personas.
expectations: Takes the lite tier — runs a single persona (Hacker), not all three, for a one-function bug review
